items.statement: I am drawn to ideas, art, or experiences that are unusual or new.
items.raw_reply: Frankly, I'm a 4. I enjoy trying new things, but I'm not out there seeking the avant-garde just for its own sake. If it's innovative tech or a new restaurant, sure, I'm interested. But I'm not one for fringe festivals or weird art just to say I've done it.

items.statement: I rarely think deeply about abstract questions.
items.raw_reply: Honestly, I'd say I'm a 4 on that. My work doesn't exactly require navel-gazing, and I've got enough on my plate without pondering the meaning of life. That being said, I do enjoy reading about history, particularly stuff like Robert Caro's LBJ biographies – that's about as abstract as I get.

items.statement: I have an active imagination and notice connections others miss.
items.raw_reply: I'd say that's a 4 for me. My job involves finding patterns and connections in financial data, and I've gotten pretty good at spotting trends that others might overlook. But I wouldn't say I've got a wildly creative imagination – it's more about applying logic and analysis to complex problems.

items.statement: I follow through on commitments and finish what I start.
items.raw_reply: That's a 5. I don't make commitments lightly, and when I do, I see them through. It's a matter of personal and professional pride for me. If I say I'm going to do something, you can bet I'll get it done.

items.statement: I often leave things half-done or in disarray.
items.raw_reply: A 1. That's just not how I operate. I'm a finisher, not a starter. If I take on a project or task, I see it through to completion, and I do it in a way that's organized and thorough. Leaving things half-done or in disarray would drive me crazy, frankly.

items.statement: I plan carefully and prefer order to chaos.
items.raw_reply: That's a 5. I'm a planner, always have been. I like to know what's coming next and have a strategy in place to tackle it. Chaos is just not my style – it's inefficient and wastes time. Give me a well-organized spreadsheet and a clear plan of action any day.

items.statement: Being around people gives me energy rather than draining me.
items.raw_reply: Honestly, I'd say a 2. People are necessary for getting things done, but they don't exactly energize me. I can handle them, I'm not antisocial or anything, but at the end of the day, I'm just as happy to be alone with my own thoughts and a good book.

items.statement: I tend to be quiet and keep to myself in groups.
items.raw_reply: A 4. I'm not going to pretend to be some charismatic socialite, frankly. items.raw_reply: A 5. Look, I'm not afraid to steer a conversation in the direction I think it should go, and if that means being the center of attention, so be it. items.statement: People who are slower than me are usually wasting my time.
items.raw_reply: A 5. Frankly, yes. I don't have time to babysit people who can't keep up. If you're not moving at my pace, you're holding me back, and that's just frustrating. I've got deals to close, meetings to get to, and a career to advance. I don't have patience for people who can't hack it.

items.statement: I find it hard to hold grudges; I forgive without much trouble.
items.raw_reply: A 4. I'm not one to dwell on things, to be honest. Holding grudges is just not productive, and it's a waste of my time. I've got better things to do than nurse a grudge. That being said, I'm not a pushover, and if someone crosses me, they'll know about it. But once it's dealt with, I'm done with it.

items.raw_reply: A 2. Look, I've got a lot on my plate, and I've learned to prioritize what actually matters.
